title: pithecellobium pubescens
intro: Pithecellobium pubescens, also known as the "Mexican ironwood" or "Puertorican ironwood", is a species of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to the Caribbean and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Pithecellobium pubescens include its potential to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, as well as its ability to act as a natural diuretic. The herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the leaves and using them in a variety of ways.
